description: Reference SoM manifest for E1M-AEN301 — Alp Lab E1M-AEN family member built around the Alif Semiconductor Ensemble E3 SoC (2x Cortex-M55 + 2x Ethos-U55, no application CPU). The E1M-AEN family has six SKUs (E1M-AEN301/401/501/601/701/801) that share this E1M routing and differ only in Ensemble variant, MRAM size, and ISP/JPEG-encoder option. Snippet is illustrative — it lists only the pads needed for debug + first I2C peripheral, not the full routing.

silicon.notes: 100 Mbps Ethernet PHY. Single PHY — only ETH0_* is routed.
CAN transceiver for CAN0_*. CAN1_* not present on E1M form factor.

routing_caveats.boot_strap: BOOT0..BOOT3 are NC on every ALP-AEN SKU. The AEN family does not support strap-driven boot-mode selection.
routing_caveats.ethernet: Only ETH0_* is routed (Ensemble has one MAC). ETH1_* pads are NC.
routing_caveats.mipi_csi: Only CSI0_* is routed (Ensemble has one CSI controller, 2 lanes).
routing_caveats.mipi_dsi: Only DSI0_* is routed (Ensemble has one DSI controller, 2 lanes).
routing_caveats.pcie: Not routed — the AEN family does not expose PCIe.
routing_caveats.usb3: Not routed — only USB 2.0 (USB2_*) is available.
routing_caveats.module_stby: Enters real-time-clock standby only — does not gate the application CPU.
